rulings.Case Title: JOESIAH BELL VS. SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL et al (Deft: Russell Challenge Judge Goldsmith)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY/RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 18, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT MARIKA RUSSELL's MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY/RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY. Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court finds that this motion is unopposed. Defendant's motion to compel answers to interrogatories and responses and production to requests for documents is granted. The court finds that plaintiff lack of a timely response to these discovery requests was not justified. Therefore, plaintiff has waived all his objections. Plaintiff to serve verified responses to interrogatories no later than March 6, 2015. Plaintiff to serve verified responses and to produce documents no later than March 6, 2015. Defendants' motion to compel plaintiff to serve a responsive statement to defendant's request for Statement of Damages pursuant to C.C.P. Section 425.11(b) is granted. Plaintiff to serve his verified response no later than March 6, 2015. Defendants request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff Joesiah Bell to pay sanctions in the amount of $630.00 ($570 in attorney fees and $60 in filing fees ) no later than March 6, 2015. Check to be made payable to defendant Marika Dubin Russell, M.D. and her attorneys of record Hassard Bonnington LLP. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA L. TANACEA VS. RICHARD R. TAVERNETTI MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Kendra Tanacea'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, Md, To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And For Order Striking Objections; Request Questions (Duplicate Entry - For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 18, 2015, Line 3, Plaintiff Kendra Tanacea'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, MD, To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And For Order Striking Objections; Request Questions (Duplicate Entry - For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only) (Part 2 of 2). The present lawsuit involves allegations that defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, M.D. recommended a hip replacement device which he had a financial interest in. In an attempt to prove the allegations, plaintiff must be able to obtain documents showing his compensation from his consulting agreement with Exactech, Inc. The documents produced shall be subject to a protective order and shall be returned and/or destroyed within 30 days of the resolution of this case or when all appeals have been exhausted. All other objections are overruled on their merits. The court finds that the responses to the requests are not in compliance with C.C.P. § 2031.210 - 240. Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, M.D. to serve a verified response and produce documents to Requests No. 1-9 no later than March 6, 2015. Response to be in compliance with C.C.P. § 2031.210 et seq. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, M.D. to pay sanctions in the amount of $3600.00 no later than March 6, 2015. Check to be made payable to Kendra L. Tanacea and her attorneys of record Injijian Law Office, APC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA L. TANACEA VS. RICHARD R. TAVERNETTI MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, Md, To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And For Order Striking Objections; Request Questions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 18, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF KENDRA TANACEA's Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, Md, To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And For Order Striking Objections; Request Questions. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses and produce documents is as follows: Request No. 1 - 9: Granted. Objection as to privacy is overruled. Personal financial information comes within the zone of privacy protected by Article I, § 1 of the California Constitution. Valley Bank of Nevada v. Superior Court (1975) 15 Cal. 3d 652, 656 Tax records are also specifically protected by statute and case law. California Revenue and Tax Code §19542 et seq.; Webb v. Standard Oil Co. (1957) 49 Cal. 2d. 509 When an individual's right of privacy in his financial affairs conflicts with the public need for discovery in litigation, the competing interests must be carefully balanced. Moskowitz v. Superior Court 137 C.A. 3d 313,316 citing Valley Bank of Nevada v. Superior Court, supra. The more sensitive the information the greater the need for discovery must be shown. Hoffman Corp. v. Superior Court (1985) 172 C. A. 3d 357 Even where the balance weighs in favor of disclosure of private information, the scope of the disclosure will be narrowly circumscribed; such an invasion of the right of privacy "must be drawn with narrow specificity" and is permitted only to the extent necessary for a fair resolution of the lawsuit. Moskowitz v. Superior Court, supra, citing Britt v. Superior Court 20 Cal. 3d 844 The case law is clear--where a party is compelled to reveal financial information because the information is relevant to the subject matter of the litigation, the court should take "a closer look at the requests with an eye toward limiting discovery to those documents where are reasonably related to lead to admissible evidence of [plaintiffs'] substantive claims." Rawnsley v. Superior Court (1986) 183 Cal. App. 3d 86. (Continued to part 2 of 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA L. TANACEA VS. RICHARD R. TAVERNETTI MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Kendra Tanacea'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, Md, To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set One, And For Order Striking Objections; Request For Sanctions (Duplicate Entry - For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 18, 2015, Line 3, Plaintiff Kendra Tanacea's Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, MD, To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set One, And For Order Striking Objections; Request For Sanctions (Duplicate Entry - For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only) (Part 2 of 2). The court further finds that defendant Tavernetti did not make the required good faith effort in responding to the interrogatories. A party must make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information in answering interrogatories. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th1496 "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under his control." Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771,782. This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Sup. Ct (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA 2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (U.Z.MFG.Co) (1984) 161 CA 3d 15,167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (Benny) (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts who have been retained by a party and designated as a trial witness. Sigerseth v. Superior Court(1972) 23 CA 3d 427,433. See Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2009) 8:1051-1060 The statutes and the case law make it very clear that a party and the attorney must be proactive in obtaining the information to respond to the interrogatories. See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 CA4th 390 Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, M.D. to serve verified responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories No. 1-20 no later than March 6, 2015. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, M.D. to pay sanctions in the amount of $4800.00 no later than March 6, 2015. Check to be made payable to Kendra L. Tanacea and her attorneys of record Injijian Law Office, APC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA L. TANACEA VS. RICHARD R. TAVERNETTI MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, Md, To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set One, And For Order Striking Objections; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 18, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF KENDRA TANACEA's Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, MD, To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set One, And For Order Striking Objections; Request For Sanctions. (Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to specially prepared interrogatories is as follows: Special Interrogatories 1- 20: Granted. Objection as to privacy is overruled. Personal financial information comes within the zone of privacy protected by Article I, § 1 of the California Constitution. Valley Bank of Nevada v. Superior Court (1975) 15 Cal. 3d 652, 656 Tax records are also specifically protected by statute and case law. California Revenue and Tax Code §19542 et seq.; Webb v. Standard Oil Co. (1957) 49 Cal. 2d. 509 When an individual's right of privacy in his financial affairs conflicts with the public need for discovery in litigation, the competing interests must be carefully balanced. Moskowitz v. Superior Court 137 C.A. 3d 313,316 citing Valley Bank of Nevada v. Superior Court, supra. The more sensitive the information the greater the need for discovery must be shown. Hoffman Corp. v. Superior Court (1985) 172 C. A. 3d 357 Even where the balance weighs in favor of disclosure of private information, the scope of the disclosure will be narrowly circumscribed; such an invasion of the right of privacy "must be drawn with narrow specificity" and is permitted only to the extent necessary for a fair resolution of the lawsuit. Moskowitz v. Superior Court, supra, citing Britt v. Superior Court 20 Cal. 3d 844 The case law is clear--where a party is compelled to reveal financial information because the information is relevant to the subject matter of the litigation, the court should take "a closer look at the requests with an eye toward limiting discovery to those documents where are reasonably related to lead to admissible evidence of [plaintiffs'] substantive claims." Rawnsley v. Superior Court (1986) 183 Cal. App. 3d 86. The present lawsuit involves allegations that defendant Richard R. Tavernetti, Md. recommended a hip replacement device which he had a financial interest in. In an attempt to prove the allegations, plaintiff must be able to discovery information showing defendant Tavernetti's compensation from his consulting agreement with Exactech, Inc. All other objections are overruled on their merits. (Continued to part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF GENEVA MACK et al VS. PACIFIC PULMONARY SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 18 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRADEN PARTNERS, L.P. DBA PACIFIC PULMONARY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained. As to the cause of action for "Products Liability - Negligence," the demurer is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action is duplicative of the negligence cause of action. (See Ferrari v. Grand Canyon Dories (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 248, 257, "product liability negligence theory is encompassed in plaintiff's separate count for negligence.") As to the cause of action for "Products Liability - Warranty," the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The complaint does not adequately allege facts establishing that any affirmation was part of the basis of the bargain. (See Hauter v. Zogarts (1975) 14 Cal.3d 104, 115.) Additionally, Plaintiffs should allege facts clarifying whether Defendant merely serviced/repaired the equipment, or whether Defendant manufactured and/or sold the equipment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN F GREENWALD et al VS. STEVEN A MINISINI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Steven Greenwald And Rochelle Alperts' Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatories To Defendant.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 18, 2015, Line 5, Plaintiffs Steven Greenwald And Rochelle Alperts' Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatories To Defendant (Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The ruling on plaintiff's motion to compel defendant Jennifer T. Chow to serve further answers to requests for admissions and Form Interrogatory #17.1 is as follows: Request for Admissions #1-23: Granted. All objections are overruled. CCP Section 2033.010 specifically states that "Any party may obtain discovery¿by a written request that any other party to the action admit the genuineness of specified documents, or the trust of specified matters of fact, opinion relating to fact, or application of law to fact. A request for admission my relate to a matter that is in controversy between the parties." A party is also obligated to perform a reasonable inquiry before answering requests for admissions. C.C.P. Section 2033.220. This requires a party must make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information when answering discovery. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th1496 "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under his control." Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771,782. This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Sup. Ct (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA 2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (U.Z.MFG.Co) (1984) 161 CA 3d 15,167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (Benny) (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts who have been retained by a party and designated as a trial witness. Sigerseth v. Superior Court(1972) 23 CA 3d 427,433. See Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2009) 8:1051-1060 The statutes and the case law make it very clear that a party and the attorney must be proactive in obtaining the information to respond discovery. See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 CA4th 390 The court finds that defendant Minisini didn't meet his obligation when he responded to the requests for admissions. Furthermore, C.C.P.Section 2033.220(b)(1) requires a party to "Admit so much of the matter involved in the request as is true, either as expressed in the request itself or as reasonably and clearly qualified by the responding party." (Continued to part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN F GREENWALD et al VS. STEVEN A MINISINI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Steven Greenwald And Rochelle Alpert'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents To Defendant.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 18, 2015, Line 5, Plaintiffs Steven Greenwald And Rochelle Alpert'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents To Defendant. Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The ruling on plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents is as follows: Requests #6-9, 12-21 and 24-25: Granted. Objections on the grounds of vague, ambiguous, overbroad, burdensome and irrelevant are overruled. There is no ruling on the objections on the grounds of attorney on the grounds of attorney-client privilege or work product doctrines as defendant Minisini failed to serve a privilege log with his response as is required by C.C.P. Section 2031.240. Defendant Steven A. Minisini to serve a further verified response and produce documents to Requests #6-9, 12-21 and 24-25 by March 6, 2015. Requests to be in compliance with C.C.P. Section 2031.210-240. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. Defendant Steven A. Minisini to pay sanctions in the amount of $1160.00 ($1100 in attorney fees and $60 in filing fees) no later than March 6, 2015. Check to be made payable to Steven F. Greenwald and Rochelle D. Alpert and their attorneys of record Moscone, Emblidge, Sater & Otis. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

Greenwald and Rochelle D. Alpert and their attorneys of record Moscone, Emblidge, Sater & Otis.
